title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praises Celtics' transformation into Eastern Conference contenders, highlighting their growth without Jayson Tatum.
Celtics swept regular-season series against Warriors, showing strong team development with younger players gaining experience.
Boston ranks second in Eastern Conference, excelling in offensive efficiency and team spacing under coach Joe Mazzulla.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates filed a lawsuit against Worcester, alleging discrimination in handling mental health 911 calls with armed police.
The lawsuit states Worcester Police send officers to all mental health crises, lacking specialized mental health response.
A 2024 DOJ report cited Worcester Police for civil rights violations, supporting advocates' claims of improper conduct.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff announced as Northeastern University's 2026 commencement speaker in Y2K-themed social media post.
Northeastern's 2026 undergraduate commencement is set for April 29 at Fenway Park.
Duff, known for 'Lizzy Maguire,' has over 56 million Spotify streams for 'What Dreams Are Made Of.'

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots seek a proven No. 1 wide receiver, with A.J. Brown as a potential trade target.
New England aims to improve its pass rush after K'Lavon Chaisson's departure, focusing on draft prospects.
The Patriots look to bolster offensive tackle depth to support and eventually replace veteran Morgan Moses.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 16-year-old from Needham has been charged with murder in the October shooting death of Marie Molea-Anthony, 61, in Roxbury.
The teen, currently in custody on unrelated charges, also faces charges of aggravated assault and unlawful possession of a firearm.
Boston police are investigating the homicide and encouraging anyone with information to contact them or use the anonymous tip line.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr. will not be retried after spending 34 years in prison for a 1985 murder conviction.
Suffolk County DA dropped charges citing new forensic testing, lost evidence, and changes in case law.
Rosa is the 100th exoneree from Massachusetts since 1989, according to the National Registry of Exonerations.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado, 35, was sentenced to 35-40 years in prison for raping his 5-year-old niece in 2022.
Machado was convicted of multiple charges including child rape, indecent assault, and drugging for sexual intercourse.
Machado was arrested in Chicago and extradited to Massachusetts after being located through financial transactions.

title: A month after tragic shooting, Colin Dorgan, Blackstone Valley hockey triumph to win R.I. state title
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Blackstone Valley co-op hockey team won the Rhode Island Division II championship against Lincoln High School in quadruple overtime.
Senior Colin Dorgan scored the tying goal in the final seconds, propelling Blackstone Valley to their first state title.
The win came a month after a tragic shooting during Senior Night, which claimed Dorgan's mother, brother, and grandfather.

title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins recalled Lukas Reichel from Providence on emergency basis ahead of game against Winnipeg Jets.
Boston acquired Reichel from Vancouver for a 2026 sixth-round pick before trade deadline.
Reichel recorded five points in three games with Providence, aiming to boost Bruins' forward line.

title: World Cup games at Gillette no longer in jeopardy after Foxborough officially approves license
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough approved an event license for seven World Cup games at Gillette Stadium, resolving security funding concerns.
Boston Soccer 2026 committed to covering $7.8 million in security costs, ensuring local taxpayers are not burdened.
The town, Kraft Sports & Entertainment, and BS26 finalized plans, including traffic management and enhanced MBTA service.
